creator: dch@freebsd.org
text:
Thanks for looking into this. In your log you see this:

2017-01-12T04:05:04.911619+08:00: [ERROR]: database error: 0: success: no error

Note that the original parent process continues to run and serve requests, only
the forked child that handles snapshots dies.

I attached dtruss.log which shows the process forking, and failing to find the snapshot file:

   890/100151:  fork(0x0, 0x0, 0x0)              = 891 0
   890/100151:  gettimeofday(0x7FFFDEBF3C30, 0x0, 0x0)           = 0 0
...
   890/100151:  lstat("0001-bgs/00000000.ktss.tmp\0", 0x7FFFDEBF3C00, 0x0)               = -1 Err#2


I think that this error corresponds to the failure of the forked process
that writes snapshots out to disk, to create the snapshot. Do you also
see ktserver.core there now too, or do you have the snapshot under 001-bgs/ ?

root@ice:/tmp # tree
.
|-- 0001-bgs
|-- 0001-ulog
|   `-- 0000000001.ulog
`-- ktserver.core

2 directories, 2 files
root@ice:/tmp #


I uploaded the 117MB ktserver coredump here https://wintermute.skunkwerks.at/~dch/FreeBSD/PR215730/ along with dtruss.log.
